options.label: A
options.text: The extension of a spring is directly proportional to the force applied to it

options.label: B
options.text: The extension of a spring is inversely proportional to the force applied to it

options.label: C
options.text: Springs always return to their original shape no matter how much force is applied

options.label: D
options.text: The force applied to a spring is always greater than the extension produced

correct_answer: The extension of a spring is directly proportional to the force applied to it
explanation: Hooke's law states that when you apply a force to a spring, it stretches (extends) by an amount that is directly proportional to the force. This means if you double the force, the spring stretches twice as much. This relationship only works up to the limit of proportionality, after which the spring may not return to its original shape.
